quiz.title: Unit 7 Quiz 1, Grade 11 Chemistry
quiz.subtitle: Molar Mass Calculations
quiz.subject: Chemistry
quiz.topic: Molar Mass, Formula Mass, Atomic Mass, Calculating Molar Mass of Compounds

questions.question: The molar mass of water (H₂O) is approximately:
The molar mass of carbon dioxide (CO₂) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Carbon dioxide (CO₂): C = 12.01 g/mol, O = 2 × 16.00 = 32.00 g/mol. Total = 44.01 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of sodium chloride (NaCl) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Sodium chloride (NaCl): Na = 22.99 g/mol, Cl = 35.45 g/mol. Total = 58.44 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of glucose (C₆H₁₂O₆) is approximately:
The molar mass of sulfuric acid (H₂SO₄) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Sulfuric acid (H₂SO₄): H = 2 × 1.008 = 2.016 g/mol, S = 32.06 g/mol, O = 4 × 16.00 = 64.00 g/mol. Total = 98.08 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of calcium carbonate (CaCO₃) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Calcium carbonate (CaCO₃): Ca = 40.08 g/mol, C = 12.01 g/mol, O = 3 × 16.00 = 48.00 g/mol. Total = 100.09 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of ammonia (NH₃) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Ammonia (NH₃): N = 14.01 g/mol, H = 3 × 1.008 = 3.024 g/mol. Total = 17.03 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of potassium permanganate (KMnO₄) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Potassium permanganate (KMnO₄): K = 39.10 g/mol, Mn = 54.94 g/mol, O = 4 × 16.00 = 64.00 g/mol. Total = 158.04 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of ethanol (C₂H₅OH) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Ethanol (C₂H₅OH): C = 2 × 12.01 = 24.02 g/mol, H = 6 × 1.008 = 6.048 g/mol, O = 16.00 g/mol. Total = 46.07 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of magnesium hydroxide (Mg(OH)₂) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Magnesium hydroxide (Mg(OH)₂): Mg = 24.31 g/mol, O = 2 × 16.00 = 32.00 g/mol, H = 2 × 1.008 = 2.016 g/mol. Total = 58.33 g/mol ≈ 58.32 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of iron(III) oxide (Fe₂O₃) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Iron(III) oxide (Fe₂O₃): Fe = 2 × 55.85 = 111.70 g/mol, O = 3 × 16.00 = 48.00 g/mol. Total = 159.70 g/mol ≈ 159.69 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of acetic acid (CH₃COOH) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Acetic acid (CH₃COOH): C = 2 × 12.01 = 24.02 g/mol, H = 4 × 1.008 = 4.032 g/mol, O = 2 × 16.00 = 32.00 g/mol. Total = 60.05 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO₃) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Sodium bicarbonate (NaHCO₃): Na = 22.99 g/mol, H = 1.008 g/mol, C = 12.01 g/mol, O = 3 × 16.00 = 48.00 g/mol. Total = 84.01 g/mol.

questions.question: The molar mass of nitrogen gas (N₂) is approximately:
The molar mass of copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate (CuSO₄·5H₂O) is approximately:
questions.explanation: Copper(II) sulfate pentahydrate (CuSO₄·5H₂O): CuSO₄ = 63.55 + 32.06 + 64.00 = 159.61 g/mol, 5H₂O = 5 × 18.02 = 90.10 g/mol. Total = 249.71 g/mol ≈ 249.68 g/mol.

scoreMessages.90: Excellent! You've mastered molar mass calculations! 🧪⚖️
scoreMessages.70: Good job! Review atomic masses and practice counting atoms in formulas.
scoreMessages.50: Satisfactory. Focus on multiplying atomic masses by the number of atoms.
scoreMessages.0: Please review how to calculate molar mass by adding the atomic masses of all atoms in the formula.
